description: In-page JS injection for complex SPA forms. Detects framework and manipulates DOM state directly when act() fails.

whenToUse: act() fails 2x on the same form field
Accessibility tree shows element but fill/click has no effect
Form uses React/Vue/Angular/Svelte with controlled components
Framework manages state externally (not in DOM attributes)
Nested shadow DOM or web components

react.description: Access React fiber tree to trigger onChange/setState

setValue.script: (() => { const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(el, VALUE); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true })); })()
setValue.notes: React 16+ controlled components. nativeInputValueSetter bypasses React's synthetic event system.

click.script: (() => { const reactPropsKey = Object.keys(el).find(k => k.startsWith('__reactProps$')); if (reactPropsKey && el[reactPropsKey]?.onClick) { el[reactPropsKey].onClick({ target: el, preventDefault: () => {}, stopPropagation: () => {} }); } el.click(); })()
click.notes: Triggers React's synthetic onClick handler directly.

select.script: (() => { const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLSelectElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(el, VALUE); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true })); })()
select.notes: For native <select> with React. For custom dropdowns, see customDropdown strategy.

checkbox.notes: React checkboxes need click + change event.

textarea.script: (() => { const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(el, VALUE); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true })); })()
textarea.notes: Same pattern as input but for textarea.

vue.description: Access Vue component instance via __vue__ or __vue_app__

setValue.script: (() => { const vueKey = Object.keys(el).find(k => k.startsWith('__vue')); if (vueKey) { const vnode = el[vueKey]; if (vnode?.component?.props?.modelValue !== undefined) { vnode.component.emit('update:modelValue', VALUE); } } const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(el, VALUE); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); })()
setValue.notes: Vue 3 composition API + fallback to native setter.

click.script: (() => { const vueKey = Object.keys(el).find(k => k.startsWith('__vue')); if (vueKey) { const vnode = el[vueKey]; if (vnode?.component?.props?.onClick) { vnode.component.emit('click'); } } el.click(); })()

angular.description: Access Angular component via ng.getComponent() or ng.probe()

setValue.script: (() => { if (window.ng && window.ng.getComponent) { const comp = window.ng.getComponent(el.closest('[ng-reflect-model]') || el); if (comp) { const modelKey = el.getAttribute('ng-reflect-model') || el.getAttribute('name'); if (comp[modelKey] !== undefined) { comp[modelKey] = VALUE; } } } const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(el, VALUE); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); })()
setValue.notes: Angular 14+ with Ivy. ng.getComponent() requires debug mode or ng.probe() fallback.

click.notes: Angular binds to native events, native click usually works.

svelte.description: Svelte compiles to vanilla JS, use native DOM events

setValue.script: (() => { const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(el, VALUE); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); })()
setValue.notes: Svelte binds via addEventListener, native events work.

solid.description: Solid uses fine-grained reactivity, native DOM events usually work

setValue.script: (() => { const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(el, VALUE); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); })()
setValue.notes: Solid compiles JSX to direct DOM manipulation. Native setter + events usually sufficient.

webcomponents.description: Pierce shadow DOM and manipulate inner elements

setValue.script: (() => { const root = el.shadowRoot || el; const inner = root.querySelector('input') || root.querySelector('[role="input"]'); if (inner) { const nativeSetter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set; nativeSetter.call(inner, VALUE); inner.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); } })()
setValue.notes: For shadow DOM components. May need to traverse multiple levels.

generic.description: Framework-agnostic fallback using native DOM API only

setValue.script: (() => { const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase(); let proto; if (tag === 'textarea') proto = window.HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype; else if (tag === 'select') proto = window.HTMLSelectElement.prototype; else proto = window.HTMLInputElement.prototype; const setter = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(proto, 'value')?.set; if (setter) setter.call(el, VALUE); else el.value = VALUE; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true })); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true })); })()
setValue.notes: Universal fallback. Uses native prototype setter to bypass any framework wrapper.

click.script: (() => { el.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('mousedown', { bubbles: true })); el.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('mouseup', { bubbles: true })); el.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true })); })()
click.notes: Full mouse event sequence for maximum compatibility.

customDropdown.description: For React Select, Ant Design Select, MUI Autocomplete, etc.
steps: Click the dropdown trigger to open options
Query options container for matching option
customDropdown.fallbackScript: (() => { const options = document.querySelectorAll('[role="option"], [class*="option"], [class*="menu-item"], li[id*="option"]'); for (const opt of options) { if (opt.textContent.trim().toLowerCase() === VALUE.toLowerCase()) { opt.click(); return true; } } return false; })()
customDropdown.notes: When framework-specific injection fails, open+type+select is the universal fallback for custom dropdowns.

strategy: Attempt 3: open-type-select for dropdowns, or escalate to user
retryPolicy.escalationThreshold: After 3 failed attempts on same field

neverInject: password fields (always use native keyboard input for security)
